programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: US Navy lieutenant Paul Booth is shot dead while sitting on a park bench. It turns out that the last people he called were his boss, his wife and Penelope Langston - a rebellious academic who also happens to be McGee's grandmother. She says that the victim was an old student of hers who kept in touch, but later reveals she was recruited to work on a special project during the Vietnam War by the head of a top-secret think tank Booth was a member of. Guest starring Lily Tomlin, with Mark Harmon and Sean Murray(n)

programme.title: Who Do You Think You Are?
programme.desc: Warwick Davis starts his research with his paternal grandmother Edith, who led to his big break at the age of 11 when she heard a radio advert. That advert was 'looking for short people to appear in Return of the Jedi', and Warwick was catapulted to stardom, continuing his acting career in the likes of Labyrinth and Willow. He takes a non-judgemental approach as he researches the family line stretching back from Edith, finding humanity and humour in some uncomfortable stories, while on his maternal side, he finds out about a postman who lived a double life(n)

programme.title: Fleabag
programme.desc: An event at Claire's work sees Fleabag's efforts to help out inevitably end in disaster, but an introduction to one of her sister's colleagues proves intriguing, while a chance meeting with a stranger is an eye-opener. Comedy written by and starring Phoebe Waller-Bridge as a woman wrestling with the demands of modern living as she spins through London grasping at anyone and anything that might keep her head above water. With Andrew Scott and Sian Clifford and a guest appearance by Kristin Scott Thomas(n)

programme.desc: As the tenth week of the competition begins, Lord sugar summons the candidates to Burlington House in Central London. Here, the remaining candidates are informed that for this week's task, they have been challenged to create and sell their own perfume, coming up with their own signature scent and design to sell to major retailers. For one team a failure to make decisions proves problematic, while on the other team a bold attempt to make their fragrance stand out does not go to plan. Back in the boardroom, one of the candidates is sent home from the competition(n)

programme.desc: Documentary series following families throughout their first weeks of parenthood, lifting the lid on one of the most joyful, yet intense, times in a family's life. In the second episode, cameras follow new parents Mo and Syler as they get to grips with round-the-clock feeding and sleep deprivation, and Paul and Craig's surrogate Mel gives birth to their twins. Elsewhere, five-year-old Ezrah and seven-year-old Elijah have to come to terms with new rules in the family home after the birth of their baby sister(n)

But as it is launched across the country, are customers ready for such a change in shopping habits? With Easter around the corner, the supply staff decide what to stock in each store and how much, while at the clothing HQ, plans are already being drawn up for this year's Christmas fashion(n)

programme.title: MasterChef: The Professionals
programme.desc: In the last programme of knockout week, the second group of five face two tough tests. First they are given three hours, with limited equipment and facilities, to produce a knockout plate that showcases their versatility and style. This dish is judged by a group of diners specialising in the street food and pop-up industry - and the contestant that wins the most votes sails through to the semi-finals. The other four are left to produce a showstopper dish in just 90 minutes - hoping it will be enough to get them through(n)

programme.title: Giri/Haji
programme.desc: Jiro kidnaps Taki and threatens to throw her from a rooftop, so Yuto and Kenzo race across London to confront the yakuza - but the Mori brothers must decide what they are willing to sacrifice in order to save the girl's life. A dejected Rodney tries to seek solace by returning to his childhood home, where his mother seems pleased to see him, while Hayashi visits Fukuhara and convinces him to attend the peace meeting between yakuza families. Conclusion of the crime thriller, starring Takehiro Hira, Yosuke Kubozuka and Kelly Macdonald(n)

programme.desc: Sheepdog trainer Katy invites three potential partners to experience a taste of country life in the Cumbrian Fells. While the weather is bleak, painter and decorator John, motorbike champion David and former footballer Carl take to herding sheep and mucking out kennels with remarkable success. Grace takes a day off from her family's dairy to visit a hobby farmer on his own turf, while Sara Cox pays a visit to Cumbrian farmer David to deliver letters from six candidates for a speed dating session(n)

programme.desc: Zoe Ball and Rylan Clark-Neal present analysis of the pro-celebrity couples' progress. Celebrity super fans are in the studio to discuss all things ballroom(n)

Hazel Irvine presents, with commentary and analysis from Steve Davis, John Parrott, Ken Doherty, Stephen Hendry, Alan McManus, John Virgo and Dennis Taylor(n)

programme.title: Mountain Vets
programme.desc: On the road, senior vet Rachael Frew heads to a dairy farm to see a cow with a suspected displaced stomach. Recognising it as a life-threatening condition, she decides to operate immediately and tries to move the stomach back into place by hand. However, it proves a tricky process and the farmer is called upon to assist. Meanwhile, specialist small animal surgeon Martin Fitzpatrick operates on young sheepdog Ellie in a bid to save her leg, which has been badly injured by some wire. Narrated by Kevin Murphy(n)

programme.title: The Name of the Rose
programme.desc: Salvatore is returned to the abbey with the Occitan girl, who is accused of witchcraft by Bernard Gui. Salvatore is tortured and confesses to Bernardo that he witnessed Remigio kill a bishop when they were both followers of the heretic Dolcino. Remigio at last confesses to being a follower of Dolcino and of still believing in the doctrine of poverty. William and the Franciscans are alarmed at the confession and of Bernard Gui linking the heretical teachings of Dolcino to their own vows of poverty. John Turturro, Rupert Everett and Greta Scarano star(n)

programme.title: Amazing Hotels: Life Beyond the Lobby
programme.desc: Giles Coren and Monica Galetti join the staff working in some of the most unusual hotels around the world, learning the tricks of the trade(n)

programme.title: Mock the Week
programme.desc: Dara O Briain looks back on the latest events in news and politics, with the help of Tom Allen, Ed Gamble, Kerry Godliman, Rhys James and Rosie Jones and regular panellist Hugh Dennis. As usual, stand-up and improvisation are the name of the game as the contestants try to get one up on one another(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events. Presented by Kirsty Wark(n)

programme.title: Snooker: UK Championship
programme.desc: Seema Jaswal presents action from this evening's quarter-finals on day 10 at the Barbican Centre in York(n)

programme.title: Beyond 100 Days
programme.desc: BBC News teams in Washington DC and London report on the events that are shaping the world(n)

programme.title: Yorkshire Walks
programme.desc: Artist Shanaz Gulzar seeks inspiration while walking a stretch of the 109-mile Cleveland Way, meeting fellow walkers along the way and finding spots to sketch and recite poetry. Starting in Runswick Bay, and armed only with a handheld 360-degree selfie-style camera. she climbs the steep steps to the cliff top before strolling along the coastal path, absorbing the beautiful landscape and its industrial past, before finishing at Whitby for sunset(n)

programme.title: Sacred Wonders of Britain
programme.desc: Neil Oliver travels to Iona in the Inner Hebrides to learn about St Columba's tough brand of monasticism, and finds out how the epic journey of St Cuthbert led to the writing of the Lindisfarne Gospels and the building of Durham Cathedral. At Canterbury Cathedral, Neil discovers how the gruesome murder of Thomas Becket spurred the building of its nave, and on Glastonbury Tor in Somerset, he investigates the story of the Holy Grail, the sacred cup of everlasting life(n)

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: This edition looks at how a lobster led archaeologists to the discovery of an 8000-year-old Neolithic underwater settlement. Meanwhile, a team at a secret location in Dorset excavate an Iron Age crouched burial, and Naoise Mac Sweeney visits a modern construction site as it gives up the secrets of an Elizabethan playhouse(n)

programme.title: Vic & Bob's Big Night Out
programme.desc: Comedy series with Vic Reeves and Bob Mortimer, featuring a welcome return for Novelty Island, a special rendition of That's Magic, as well as an appearance from Michael Portillo as you have never seen him before(n)

programme.title: Mortimer & Whitehouse: Gone Fishing
programme.desc: Cameras follow old friends Paul Whitehouse and Bob Mortimer as they continue on their angling trip round the UK. In the Monsal Valley in Derbyshire, Paul tries to teach Bob how to fly fish for wild rainbow trout, and the pair reflect on their humble beginnings, their careers and their comedy contemporaries. Later, Bob cooks Paul his very personal recipe of tuna melanie on the river bank, and it soon transpires his cooking is a lot more successful than his fishing(n)

programme.title: Mortimer & Whitehouse: Gone Fishing
programme.desc: Cameras follow old friends Paul Whitehouse and Bob Mortimer as they continue on their angling trip round the UK. This time, Paul and Bob fish the river Wensum in Norfolk for Roach, but as the pair struggle up and down the riverbanks, they begin to realise how age is starting to take its toll on their physicality. After staying overnight in a disused train station and visiting a local pub, the pair decide to challenge each other to a fishing competition the following day, where the winner gets a very special culinary treat(n)

programme.title: Thailand: Earth's Tropical Paradise
programme.desc: Exploring the wildlife and culture of the Southeast Asian nation. The first episode examines the south of the country, a popular tourist destination for Westerners, and examines the role of spirituality in daily life. Narrated by Sophie Okonedo(n)

programme.title: Masters of the Pacific Coast: The Tribes of the American Northwest
programme.desc: Part two of two. Archaeologist Dr Jago Cooper reveals how the American Northwest cultural tradition managed to survive against the odds. After European contact, indigenous people of what is now southwest Alaska, British Colombia and Washington State suffered disease, theft and oppression, and Cooper argues that Northwest Coast culture has an extraordinary resilience(n)

programme.title: The High Art of the Low Countries
programme.desc: Andrew Graham-Dixon tours the Low Countries, exploring how history has influenced the area's art, architecture and culture. In the first edition, he details how the art of Renaissance Flanders evolved from the crafting of precious tapestries, and begins his journey at the altarpiece of Ghent Cathedral, created by the Van Eyck brothers, explaining their groundbreaking innovation in oil painting and marvels at how the colours are still vibrant today. Plus, he describes how during the era people believed they were preoccupied with the end of the world(n)

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: This edition looks at how a lobster led archaeologists to the discovery of an 8000-year-old Neolithic underwater settlement. Meanwhile, a team at a secret location in Dorset excavate an Iron Age crouched burial, and Naoise Mac Sweeney visits a modern construction site as it gives up the secrets of an Elizabethan playhouse(n)

programme.title: Beyond 100 Days
programme.desc: BBC News teams in Washington DC and London report on the events that are shaping the world(n)

programme.title: Yorkshire Walks
programme.desc: Artist Shanaz Gulzar walks through the Bolton Abbey Estate, heading out from Bolton Priory along the River Wharfe and over moorland to her journey's end at Simon's Seat and its views across Wharfedale, Nidderdale and beyond. Meeting fellow walkers along the way, Shanaz finds inspirational spots to sketch and recite poetry, while discovering hidden woodlands and waterfalls(n)

programme.title: Wonders of the Solar System
programme.desc: Brian Cox compares the geography of Earth and Mars to understand why life is abundant on this planet, while its neighbour is a geologically dead desert world of ice and dust. Investigating the importance of size, he contrasts the Grand Canyon and Hawaii's Mauna Kea mountain with the 17km-high Olympus Mons peak on the Martian surface and studies why Venus - often called Earth's twin planet - was made barren by huge volcanic eruptions(n)

programme.title: Wild Weather with Richard Hammond
programme.desc: The host continues his investigation into the secrets of weather, focusing on the crucial role played by water, without which there would be no rain, no hail and no clouds. Mixing experiments with contributions from scientists, Richard goes in search of water in all its forms, discovering how rain could crush a car, trying to build his own cloud, getting involved in starting an avalanche and finding out how something as fragile as a snowflake can travel at speeds of up to 250mph on the ground(n)

programme.title: Horizon
programme.desc: Documentary following scientists conducting research into weather on other planets, using unmanned spacecraft to study the solar system and the latest telescopes to examine distant worlds. Their findings have revealed strange phenomena including rains of precious stones, supersonic winds, storms that encompass entire worlds, and temperatures so hot that the clouds are formed of lava(n)

programme.title: Wild West: America's Great Frontier
programme.desc: Documentary exploring the history and geography of America's deserts, canyons and wilderness, exploring the lives of the people and animals that have found ways to thrive and prosper in these brutally inhospitable conditions. The first programme examines the agricultural traditions of the Hopi Native American tribe in northeastern Arizona, as well as exploring the strategies used by a variety of wildlife, including mustangs, coyotes and desert tortoises, to survive in these demanding environments(n)

programme.title: Letters from Baghdad (2016)
programme.desc: The extraordinary and dramatic story of Gertrude Bell, who helped to shape the modern Middle East after the Second World War in ways that still reverberate today. Using never-seen-before footage of the region, the film chronicles Bell's extraordinary journey into both the uncharted Arabian desert and the inner sanctum of British male colonial power(n)

programme.title: Handmade in the Pacific
programme.desc: An insight into the creative processes and cultural significance of traditional artefacts from the Pacific region, beginning with making of an Aboriginal didgeridoo(n)

programme.title: Handmade in the Pacific
programme.desc: One of the last traditional weavers on the Polynesian island of Rurutu creates a ceremonial taupoo - an ornate hat worn on special occasions - from dried pandanus leaves. Taking around five weeks to make, these hats were originally introduced to the island by British missionaries in the early 1800s, and now are worn to church and given as wedding gifts(n)

programme.title: Wonders of the Solar System
programme.desc: Brian Cox compares the geography of Earth and Mars to understand why life is abundant on this planet, while its neighbour is a geologically dead desert world of ice and dust. Investigating the importance of size, he contrasts the Grand Canyon and Hawaii's Mauna Kea mountain with the 17km-high Olympus Mons peak on the Martian surface and studies why Venus - often called Earth's twin planet - was made barren by huge volcanic eruptions(n)

programme.desc: The day's leading stories(n)

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1988
programme.desc: Bruno Brookes and Mike Read present the edition first aired on December 1, featuring performances by Rick Astley, Michael Jackson, Pet Shop Boys, George Michael, Angry Anderson, Cliff Richard, Phil Collins, Bros, Robin Beck and Humanoid(n)

programme.title: Sound of Song
programme.desc: As his exploration of great songs reaches the modern era, Neil Brand charts how the arrival of digital technology and the computer transformed music. He talks synthesisers with Yes keyboardist Rick Wakeman, discusses sampling with Public Enemy's chief producer Hank Shocklee, uncovers the surprising lo-fi origins of Bruce Springsteen's Born in the USA, and finds out how Cher changed her voice on the smash hit Believe(n)

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1988
programme.desc: Nicky Campbell and Caron Keating present the edition first aired on December 8 featuring performances by Status Quo, Angry Anderson, Phil Collins, Petula Clark, New Order, Inner City, Erasure, Kylie Minogue and Jason Donovan, Cliff Richard and Bananarama(n)

programme.title: Country Music by Ken Burns
programme.desc: The history of the art form and how it evolved between 1964 and 1968. During a time of cultural upheaval, country music reflected a changing America, as Loretta Lynn addresseed women's concerns, Merle Haggard championed the common man, and audiences looked beyond skin colour to embrace Charley Pride. Meanwhile, Johnny Cash descended into the chaos of drug addiction, though The Man in Black found salvation through the intervention of June Carter and a landmark album that critics still applaud. Narrated by Peter Coyote(n)

programme.title: Country Music by Ken Burns
programme.desc: How, between 1968 and 1972, country music responded to a US nation divided by the Vietnam War. Army captain-turned-songwriter Kris Kristofferson set a new lyrical standard, and artists like Bob Dylan and the Byrds found a recording home in Nashville. Plus, a hippie combo from California, the Nitty Gritty Band, created a landmark album that bridged the gap between the generations. Narrated by Peter Coyote(n)

programme.title: Country Kings at the BBC
programme.desc: A chronological compilation of performances from the BBC archive by male country singers, ranging from the Everly Brothers and Jerry Lee Lewis to Garth Brooks and Willie Nelson. Featuring classic songs from studio shows by Glen Campbell, Charley Pride, George Hamilton IV, Kenny Rogers, Clint Black, Johnny Cash and Eric Church(n)

programme.title: Our Food, Our Family with Michela Chiappa
programme.desc: The Welsh-Italian cook heads to Snowdonia to hear the real-life Romeo and Juliet story of Vincenzo Algieri and Betty Evans - two star-crossed lovers who, despite being on opposite sides of the Second World War, found love together against all the odds. From different cultures and speaking different languages, Vincenzo and Betty bonded over food - traditional Italian dishes the family still treasures to this day. Grandson Huw has some clues of the tale the lies within his family history, but hopes Michela's specialist knowledge can help fill in some of the blanks(n)

programme.title: The Falkirk Cowboys
programme.desc: The story of the amateur cowboy films made by Falkirk factory workers in the 1970s, and the impact that these films has on their community. The groups' series of increasingly ambitious films not only captured something of their time, but also communicated the creativity, fun and camaraderie within the group, providing them with an arena where they could let their imaginations run free outside of their long shifts in monotonous jobs(n)

programme.title: The Apprentice
programme.desc: As the tenth week of the competition begins, Lord sugar summons the candidates to Burlington House in Central London. Here, the remaining candidates are informed that for this week's task, they have been challenged to create and sell their own perfume, coming up with their own signature scent and design to sell to major retailers. programme.title: Choose Life: Edinburgh's Battle Against Aids
programme.desc: Documentary examining the period between 1979 and 1989 when Edinburgh faced a frightening new epidemic and was dubbed the Aids capital of Europe. programme.title: Who Do You Think You Are?
programme.desc: Warwick Davis starts his research with his paternal grandmother Edith, who led to his big break at the age of 11 when she heard a radio advert. That advert was 'looking for short people to appear in Return of the Jedi', and Warwick was catapulted to stardom, continuing his acting career in the likes of Labyrinth and Willow. He takes a non-judgemental approach as he researches the family line stretching back from Edith, finding humanity and humour in some uncomfortable stories, while on his maternal side, he finds out about a postman who lived a double life(n)

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: As Prince Andrew steps back from his public duties, Darragh MacIntyre hears from the victims of is former friend and convicted child sex offender Jeffrey Epstein. The Duke of York has been widely criticised for not expressing sympathy for these women during his television interview with the BBC's Newsnight programme. Virginia Roberts Giuffre claims she was trafficked by Epstein and forced to have sex with the prince, something he denies. In her first interview on British TV, she tells her story and reveals new details about her time with Epstein. Reporter Darragh MacIntyre also looks at another of the duke's friends, Ghislaine Maxwell, and investigates her alleged role in Epstein's prolific sex offending. As the FBI investigation continues, Panorama asks what next in the scandal of Epstein and the prince?(n)

programme.desc: Donnie MacLeod is in Geirinis sorting out a problem with rising damp. programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. programme.desc: Ali Hamidi, Neil Spooner and Tom Dove's mission to track down the world's largest carp takes them to Slovenia, visiting a remote lake full of submerged trees and a park lake that produced the country's record catch of almost 80lb(n)

programme.title: Animal Mums
programme.desc: How animals raise their young, revealing how elephants and vervet monkeys will work together to look after all the youngsters in a social group. The programme also features a lioness with eight boisterous cubs and sheep farmers who reveal how lambs learn to recognise their mothers' voices, reveals how gannets and seals find their way back to their offspring, and demonstrates some bad behaviour among young goats and orangutans(n)

Local lad Tim Hogarth likes the look of an Evel Knievel stunt action toy that lands on his table, Michael Hogben tries to get his hands on a seller's photographic press, and there is plenty of gold to keep the dealers happy(n)

programme.title: The Switch
programme.desc: Quiz in which players can accumulate big cash totals by answering super quick-fire easy money questions. However, in order to take home that cash, they must survive The Switch, a series of three super hard multiple-choice questions that each has only one incorrect choice. Hosted by Sanjeev Bhaskar(n)

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot(n)

programme.title: Cash Trapped
programme.desc: Quiz hosted by Bradley Walsh in which six contestants battle it out across a series of fast-paced question rounds where the players must actively try to sabotage their opponents by beating them to the buzzer and trapping them out of the game. In the final round, the person who has earned the most cash will go head to head against the rest of the group to try to take their winnings home(n)

programme.title: Gino's Italian Escape
programme.desc: Gino D'Acampo visits the jewel of the Italian Riviera - the Cinque Terre - five towns that are only accessible by boat or train. He begins in the smallest town, Corniglia, where he meets an opera-singing florist, before hopping on the train to Manarola and the dry-stone wallers responsible for keeping the towns from crumbling into the sea. As he heads toward the end, he also heading to Vernazza, Gino meets wine producers who scale 1,000m above sea level to tend to their vines(n)

programme.title: I'm a Celebrity Get Me Out of Here!
programme.desc: The competition is getting tougher as the celebrity campmates get closer to one of them being crowned King or Queen of the Jungle. Ant and Dec present the highlights of the past 24 hours around the camp fire. With assorted challenges testing the contestants, and homesickness taking its toll, tensions are running high, but with the end in sight, the stars try to keep their eyes on the prize(n)

programme.title: Piers Morgan's Life Stories
programme.desc: Harry Redknapp talks about his life and career as one of the most respected and experienced football managers in the modern game, as well as his victorious I'm a Celebrity appearance in 2018, when he was crowned King of the Jungle. He discusses the many twists and turns of his rollercoaster life, including a road accident in 1990 in which his friend Brian Tiler died. Harry also opens up about a dramatic dawn raid on his house and the death threats he received when he became manager of Southampton(n)

programme.desc: Harry Hill narrates a comical selection of mishaps filmed by viewers, featuring everything from giddy grandmas, cute kids and crazy animals to below-the-belt mishaps, practical jokers and painful-looking accidents(n)

programme.title: You've Been Framed!
programme.desc: Comedian Harry Hill narrates a selection of festive camcorder calamities and mobile phone footage, featuring rocking around the Christmas tree and five ways to kill a snowman(n)

programme.title: You've Been Framed!
programme.desc: Harry Hill narrates a comical selection of camcorder clips with a festive theme. A pensioner throws fireworks at carol singers, a Christmas fairy goes up in flames, and a smartphone is found inside a turkey. Plus, one unfortunate youngster receives a Christmas gift that keeps on giving(n)

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Brian seeks out old flame Tracy, afraid he may have lost his last chance at love. To his surprise, she reveals he fathered her son Dylan, and drops the boy off at the Griffins' house. However, Brian's attitude toward parenting leaves Lois and Peter ill at ease with the latest addition to the family(n)

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Brian and Jillian move in together, but Stewie interferes with their relationship. Meanwhile, Meg and Chris get jobs at a local convenience store, but she finds herself facing unfair treatment in the workplace. With the voice of Drew Barrymore(n)

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Joe begins to feel his disability is holding Bonnie back from living a full life, and decides to get a leg transplant. Excited about his new ability to walk, he takes up extreme sports, ditching his current friends for more active ones, and upsetting a jilted Peter(n)

programme.title: I'm a Celebrity: Extra Camp
programme.desc: Fresh from a night in luxury the two latest celebrities to leave the jungle are live in the studio with Joel Dommett, Emily Atack and Adam Thomas. The ex-campmates are on hand as the series creeps ever closer to crowning this year's King or Queen of the jungle. Plus, an exclusive behind-the-scenes look at the latest terrifying trial(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Ken and Denise have a wonderful night together, Percy assures Maureen he is not after Maud's money, and Mavis gets upset with gossip about Derek taking money from kids(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Mike gets an unexpected tax demand, and Alma refuses to let him touch her money. Percy and Maud go on an OAP outing, and Reg roots through her belongings in search of a will(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: The emergency services continue the search for Alice, Zoe is worried about Frank when she sees that Kim is staying at Home Farm, and Michael and Eric row about Elizabeth(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Chris is grateful that the crash brought Kathy and him back together, and Zoe warns Kim not to mess Frank around. The village children struggle emotionally with what has happened(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: The police investigate the murder of an ex-army recluse and archaeological enthusiast and suspect a building dispute could provide a motive, while Dennis asks Liz for advice when he begins to notice a change in Jenny's behaviour. Vernon decides to play Cupid on behalf of his relative Gareth and sets him up on a blind date with Rosie, but does not count on the mechanic having another admirer. Rural drama starring Duncan Bell, Aislin McGuckin and Vanessa Hehir(n)

programme.title: On the Buses
programme.desc: Jack and the inspector try to fix a water heater, but their efforts end in disaster. Comedy, starring Stephen Lewis and Bob Grant(n)

programme.title: On the Buses
programme.desc: The inspector's mother comes to stay, and proves to be a thoroughly unpleasant person. Seventies comedy, starring Stephen Lewis and Bob Grant(n)

programme.title: Man About the House
programme.desc: Chrissy wonders whether she is desirable, and Robin's lack of response leaves her downhearted. Comedy, starring Paula Wilcox and Richard O'Sullivan(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: The sleuthing spinster meets fellow enthusiasts Tommy and Tuppence at a nursing home, where the ramblings of an elderly resident prompt them to join forces and investigate the unsolved mystery of a murdered child. The trail leads to the Norfolk village of Farrell St Edmund, a community harbouring sinister secrets and tales of heartbreak. Geraldine McEwan heads an all-star cast, including Greta Scacchi, Anthony Andrews, Charles Dance, June Whitfield and Claire Bloom(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Merton investigates the murder of a retired policeman who was about to flee the village for Spain and, after visiting the victim's widow, discovers the man kept more than a few secrets from his wife. Vernon decides to arrange a poker night in the hope it will make him easy money, but doesn't count on playing against a hustler. Rural drama starring Duncan Bell, Geoffrey Hughes and Mark Jordon(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: The village is in a solemn mood as the day of the funerals arrives, Rachel blames Joe for Mark's death, and Elsa tells Eric that she is going for custody of Alice(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: The hillside is covered with flowers and people paying their respects, Turner and Caroline worry about Nick's state of mind, and Alan has a surprise for Shirley(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Audrey tries to find out all the gossip about Ken and Denise, Don worries that she will tell Barlow about the phone calls, and Mike throws his money around at the pub(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Maureen gets fed up with Reg going on about wills, Bernard quizzes people about Emily's breakdown, and Rita realises Sally is worried about the money(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: A blizzard forces the sleuthing spinster to spend a night in isolated Sittaford House, situated on a Devon moor. Without explanation, the building's owner, up-and-coming politician Clive Trevelyan, decides to spend the evening in a local hotel - only for a Ouija board to predict his murder. Mystery drama with an all-star cast, including Geraldine McEwan, Robert Hardy, Matthew Kelly, Patricia Hodge and Mel Smith(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Merton investigates the murder of a retired policeman who was about to flee the village for Spain and, after visiting the victim's widow, discovers the man kept more than a few secrets from his wife. Vernon decides to arrange a poker night in the hope it will make him easy money, but doesn't count on playing against a hustler. Rural drama starring Duncan Bell, Geoffrey Hughes and Mark Jordon(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Part two of two. The Belgian sleuth's Cornish holiday comes to a dramatic conclusion as he unravels the mystery behind the series of disastrous near-misses that placed the life of a beautiful heiress in danger, and as the truth comes to light, a secret engagement is revealed. David Suchet stars, with Philip Jackson and Hugh Fraser(n)

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: Obsessive-compulsive Mrs Tishell returns to the village, but her affection for Martin quickly disappears when he refuses to pass her fit for work. As if he didn't have enough on his plate, the grumpy doctor must also face his fear of blood when tests show that Lorna has a high level of ferritin, which is placing a strain on her heart and liver. Meanwhile, PC Penhale hopes to be selected for an elite police squad by taking part in a survival training course - but ends up shooting himself in the foot(n)

programme.title: Endeavour
programme.desc: Morse meets his intellectual match when a serial killer haunting the streets of Oxford keeps himself one step ahead of the law and leaves cryptic messages to goad the police. At first glance, it seems to be the work of a deranged lunatic, but the elaborate staging of the crimes suggests method in the madness - and a macabre sharing of the sleuth's passion for opera. Starring Shaun Evans, with Roger Allam, Anton Lesser, Sean Rigby and James Bradshaw(n)

programme.title: Safe House
programme.desc: Tom makes a huge breakthrough in the case when he links shady estate agent Roger Lane to a number of locations related to the previous abductions. Jane arrests Lane and grills him on his whereabouts - and Simon Duke even picks him out of an identity parade. Have they finally got their man? Meanwhile, after Dani gets herself into trouble, the shocking reason for her mistrust of John is finally revealed. Crime thriller, starring Stephen Moyer, Sunetra Sarker, Jason Watkins, Ashley Walters and Sacha Parkinson(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: When a pub landlady is crushed to death by a giant box during a magic show organised by famous illusionist Gideon Latimer, pathologist Kate discovers the cables had been deliberately sabotaged. Evangelising curate Andrew Maplin claims the tragedy is God's vengeance for the pagan traditions still held deep in Midsomer Oaks, but Barnaby and Nelson fear Gideon was the real target. Neil Dudgeon, Gwilym Lee and Tamzin Malleson star in the murder mystery, with Joe Absolom, Andrew-Lee Potts, Justin Salinger, Amanda Burton and Stephanie Leonidas(n)

programme.title: On the Buses
programme.desc: The company mounts an advertising campaign to attract more passengers, and launches a contest to find the perfect bus driver. Reg Varney stars(n)

programme.title: On the Buses
programme.desc: Two women vie for the inspector's affections convinced the stars foretell love. Classic Seventies comedy, starring Stephen Lewis and Bob Grant(n)

programme.title: On the Buses
programme.desc: Inspector Blake gives Jack a new clippie, knowing her incessant chatter will drive him barmy. Seventies comedy, starring Bob Grant and Stephen Lewis(n)

programme.title: Man About the House
programme.desc: Robin gets the chance to play for his college football team - but a cold threatens to earn him an early bath. Flat-share comedy, starring Richard O'Sullivan(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: A blizzard forces the sleuthing spinster to spend a night in isolated Sittaford House, situated on a Devon moor. Mystery drama with an all-star cast, including Geraldine McEwan, Robert Hardy, Matthew Kelly, Patricia Hodge and Mel Smith(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: First of a two-part storyline. Jenny's life starts to spiral out of control and begins to affect her work, leaving Dennis and Liz facing difficult decisions. New doctor Helen Trent arrives in Aidensfield with her wealthy husband Matt, hoping to make a fresh start, but finds it hard to adjust to the slow pace of life in the country. Sophie Ward joins the regular cast, including Sarah Tansey, Duncan Bell and Aislin McGuckin(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Annie lies unconscious in hospital while Mrs Bates watches over her, and Jack struggles with her condition. Alan is annoyed that Shirley did not take his proposal seriously(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Jack and Sarah are concerned when Robert doesn't want to go to school, Frank is not enthusiastic about Zoe's plans, and Alan worries about the wedding(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Mike is annoyed when Mark tells him he does not want him at his school open day, and Maud suggests Percy and Emily plan a double wedding to save money(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Ken takes Denise to a barn dance at the school, Alf tells Audrey he's sick of her showing him up, and Emily informs Rita about Bernard's desertion(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: A gathering at the Devon estate of Miss Marple's old school friend Lady Tressilian leads to murder when the eccentric Mr Treves tells the story of an unconvicted child killer. Miss Marple is on hand to assist Supt Mallard of the local constabulary, but not before a second victim is claimed. Mystery, starring Geraldine McEwan, with guest appearances by Eileen Atkins, Tom Baker, Paul Nicholls, Alan Davies and Greg Wise, with a cameo by former British number-one tennis player Greg Rusedski(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: First of a two-part storyline. Jenny's life starts to spiral out of control and begins to affect her work, leaving Dennis and Liz facing difficult decisions. New doctor Helen Trent arrives in Aidensfield with her wealthy husband Matt, hoping to make a fresh start, but finds it hard to adjust to the slow pace of life in the country. Sophie Ward joins the regular cast, including Sarah Tansey, Duncan Bell and Aislin McGuckin(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The Belgian detective is summoned to a hotel by a mysterious veiled lady who tells him that her forthcoming marriage is under threat because she is being blackmailed over a love letter. Drama, starring David Suchet and Frances Barber(n)

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: Hypochondriac Malcolm Rayner is struggling with his breathing and Martin asks him if he has ever worked with asbestos. Penhale then causes pandemonium by putting up warning notices about the substance's harmful properties throughout the village. Meanwhile, Bert pops the question to Jennifer, and Ruth finds fault with Al's business proposal. Guest starring Christopher Fairbank (Auf Wiedersehen, Pet)(n)

programme.title: Endeavour
programme.desc: A royal visit to a family owned munitions factory puts a spring in the step of Chief Superintendent Bright, but Morse is still stuck on general duties and excluded from the event. However, when an unpopular worker is found murdered in a secluded area of the shop floor, the detective finds himself drawn into the affairs of the company and its dysfunctional owners. The prime suspect is a colleague who had fallen out with the victim over an industrial accident, and Bright is eager for the investigation to be carried out as quickly and quietly as possible, but Endeavour is not convinced the case can be solved so easily. Shaun Evans stars, with Martin Jarvis, Jenny Seagrove and Craig Parkinson(n)

programme.title: Safe House
programme.desc: Sam confronts Tom and forces him to come clean about why he left the police force, only for his revelation to completely change the way she sees the man she loves. But even as the couple are coming to terms with this latest development, Simon and Liam Duke arrive at the safe house - closely followed by the Crow. Can Jane and Vedder put the pieces together in time to come to their aid? Stephen Moyer stars in the crime thriller, with Zoe Tapper, Jason Watkins and Sunetra Sarker(n)

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: First-ever episode of the popular crime drama, starring John Thaw as author Colin Dexter's Oxford detective who treats his cases as intellectual challenges. In this mystery, Morse is introduced to trusty Sergeant Lewis (Kevin Whately) and takes it upon himself to investigate the apparent suicide of a lady friend. Co-starring Gemma Jones, Patrick Troughton, James Grout and Peter Woodthorpe(n)

programme.title: On the Buses
programme.desc: Jack suggests Mum should take on the job of cook at the depot canteen. Classic comedy, starring Bob Grant and Stephen Lewis(n)

programme.title: On the Buses
programme.desc: Jack and Blakey turn out to be the only entrants in a gardening competition organised by the depot manager's wife. Seventies comedy, starring Bob Grant(n)

programme.title: Man About the House
programme.desc: The rent money disappears, forcing Jo to consider selling a prized possession. Flat-share comedy, with Sally Thomsett, Paula Wilcox and Richard O'Sullivan(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: A gathering at the Devon estate of Miss Marple's old school friend Lady Tressilian leads to murder when the eccentric Mr Treves tells the story of an unconvicted child killer. Miss Marple is on hand to assist Supt Mallard of the local constabulary, but not before a second victim is claimed. Mystery, starring Geraldine McEwan, with guest appearances by Eileen Atkins, Tom Baker, Paul Nicholls, Alan Davies and Greg Wise, with a cameo by former British number-one tennis player Greg Rusedski(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Conclusion of a two-part storyline. A kidnap case leaves Vernon's plans for Liz and Ben's wedding in chaos, while Helen's dreams of a fresh start for her and Matt look set to be shattered. Jenny is relieved when she finally gets a medical diagnosis explaining her recent symptoms, but the news brings a difficult decision for husband Dennis, who has to choose between his wife and his police career. Starring newcomer Sophie Ward, Oliver Montgomery, Duncan Bell and Sarah Tansey(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Frank has a rough night thinking about Chris's injuries, Kim and Frank talk about fixing their relationship, and Gavin dumps Lynn very publicly in the Woolpack(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Kathy feels trapped by Chris, Alan wants Amos to be his best man, Jack agrees to get involved with the heritage farm, and Vic sees a looter at the plane crash site(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Denise accuses Don of starting the phone calls again and threatens to go to the police. Alf asks Rita to be his Mayoress, and Emily tells Percy her wedding is off(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Raquel spoils Des on his birthday, Kevin tells Derek that his car is on its last legs, and Jim is amazed when Mike tells him he can borrow the Jag to take Liz to the races(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: Geraldine McEwan makes her last appearance as the spinster sleuth before handing over the knitting needles to Julia McKenzie. Following the death of old acquaintance Mr Rafiel, Miss Marple receives instructions drawn up prior to his demise asking her to embark on a coach tour to investigate an unspecified possible crime. It soon transpires that many of her companions are also travelling at his invitation and they are all in some way connected to a girl who went missing from a nun's sanctuary during World War Two, supposedly to be with her German lover, Rafiel's son Michael. Murder mystery, with a star-studded supporting cast including Richard E Grant, Johnny Briggs, Amanda Burton, Anne Reid, Ronni Ancona, George Cole, Will Mellor and Laura Michelle Kelly(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Conclusion of a two-part storyline. A kidnap case leaves Vernon's plans for Liz and Ben's wedding in chaos, while Helen's dreams of a fresh start for her and Matt look set to be shattered. Jenny is relieved when she finally gets a medical diagnosis explaining her recent symptoms, but the news brings a difficult decision for husband Dennis, who has to choose between his wife and his police career. Starring newcomer Sophie Ward, Oliver Montgomery, Duncan Bell and Sarah Tansey(n)

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: The doctor becomes convinced something is wrong with him following a conversation with his mother, and Louisa's attempts at getting him to open up about the loss of his father prove futile. Mrs Tishell goes to the surgery for advice regarding a pain in her jaw, only to find herself falling in love with Martin all over again. Elsewhere, Al receives a visit from two military policemen who are trying to track down Mike Pruddy(n)

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: Louisa decides to leave for Spain with baby James and says an awkward goodbye to Martin, but when he finds the scan of her brain, he spots a malformation and realises she could suffer a haemorrhage if she gets on the plane. Will the doctor make it to the airport in time? Meanwhile, Margaret reveals the real reason she has come to Portwenn, and disaster strikes at Bert and Jennifer's engagement party when Caroline receives an electric shock(n)

programme.title: Endeavour
programme.desc: As the sleuth studies for his forthcoming sergeant's exam, he investigates a hit-and-run that claimed the life of an eminent Oxford professor. The victim had been at odds with his peers over the fate of a potentially lucrative parcel of college-owned land, and the case is complicated by the appearance of DI Thursday's old gangland enemy - whom he must face alone while Endeavour makes a trip back to Lincolnshire to visit his ailing father(n)

programme.title: The Street
programme.desc: Stephen Graham plays Shay, a man long in the grip of alcoholism, who learns he has a 16-year-old son he knew nothing about. A further shock is in store when he discovers the lad has Down's syndrome - and he responds by refusing to have any contact. However, the youth may be Shay's only hope after so many years of hitting the bottle. Also starring Maxine Peake, Ruth Jones and Leon Harrop(n)

programme.desc: The village is in a solemn mood as the day of the funerals arrives, Rachel blames Joe for Mark's death, and Elsa tells Eric that she is going for custody of Alice(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: The hillside is covered with flowers and people paying their respects, Turner and Caroline worry about Nick's state of mind, and Alan has a surprise for Shirley(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Merton investigates the murder of a retired policeman who was about to flee the village for Spain and, after visiting the victim's widow, discovers the man kept more than a few secrets from his wife. Vernon decides to arrange a poker night in the hope it will make him easy money, but doesn't count on playing against a hustler. Rural drama starring Duncan Bell, Geoffrey Hughes and Mark Jordon(n)

programme.title: On the Buses
programme.desc: Two women vie for the inspector's affections convinced the stars foretell love. Classic Seventies comedy, starring Stephen Lewis and Bob Grant(n)

programme.title: On the Buses
programme.desc: Inspector Blake gives Jack a new clippie, knowing her incessant chatter will drive him barmy. Seventies comedy, starring Bob Grant and Stephen Lewis(n)

programme.title: Man About the House
programme.desc: Robin gets the chance to play for his college football team - but a cold threatens to earn him an early bath. Flat-share comedy, starring Richard O'Sullivan(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: A blizzard forces the sleuthing spinster to spend a night in isolated Sittaford House, situated on a Devon moor. Without explanation, the building's owner, up-and-coming politician Clive Trevelyan, decides to spend the evening in a local hotel - only for a Ouija board to predict his murder. Mystery drama with an all-star cast, including Geraldine McEwan, Robert Hardy, Matthew Kelly, Patricia Hodge and Mel Smith(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: First of a two-part storyline. Jenny's life starts to spiral out of control and begins to affect her work, leaving Dennis and Liz facing difficult decisions. New doctor Helen Trent arrives in Aidensfield with her wealthy husband Matt, hoping to make a fresh start, but finds it hard to adjust to the slow pace of life in the country. Sophie Ward joins the regular cast, including Sarah Tansey, Duncan Bell and Aislin McGuckin(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: A gathering at the Devon estate of Miss Marple's old school friend Lady Tressilian leads to murder when the eccentric Mr Treves tells the story of an unconvicted child killer. Mystery, starring Geraldine McEwan, with guest appearances by Eileen Atkins, Tom Baker, Paul Nicholls, Alan Davies and Greg Wise, with a cameo by former British number-one tennis player Greg Rusedski(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The Belgian detective is summoned to a hotel by a mysterious veiled lady who tells him that her forthcoming marriage is under threat because she is being blackmailed over a love letter. Drama, starring David Suchet and Frances Barber(n)

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: Hypochondriac Malcolm Rayner is struggling with his breathing and Martin asks him if he has ever worked with asbestos. Penhale then causes pandemonium by putting up warning notices about the substance's harmful properties throughout the village. Meanwhile, Bert pops the question to Jennifer, and Ruth finds fault with Al's business proposal. Guest starring Christopher Fairbank (Auf Wiedersehen, Pet)(n)

programme.title: Endeavour
programme.desc: A royal visit to a family owned munitions factory puts a spring in the step of Chief Superintendent Bright, but Morse is still stuck on general duties and excluded from the event. However, when an unpopular worker is found murdered in a secluded area of the shop floor, the detective finds himself drawn into the affairs of the company and its dysfunctional owners. The prime suspect is a colleague who had fallen out with the victim over an industrial accident, and Bright is eager for the investigation to be carried out as quickly and quietly as possible, but Endeavour is not convinced the case can be solved so easily. Shaun Evans stars, with Martin Jarvis, Jenny Seagrove and Craig Parkinson(n)

programme.title: Safe House
programme.desc: Sam confronts Tom and forces him to come clean about why he left the police force, only for his revelation to completely change the way she sees the man she loves. But even as the couple are coming to terms with this latest development, Simon and Liam Duke arrive at the safe house - closely followed by the Crow. Can Jane and Vedder put the pieces together in time to come to their aid? Stephen Moyer stars in the crime thriller, with Zoe Tapper, Jason Watkins and Sunetra Sarker(n)

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: First-ever episode of the popular crime drama, starring John Thaw as author Colin Dexter's Oxford detective who treats his cases as intellectual challenges. In this mystery, Morse is introduced to trusty Sergeant Lewis (Kevin Whately) and takes it upon himself to investigate the apparent suicide of a lady friend. Co-starring Gemma Jones, Patrick Troughton, James Grout and Peter Woodthorpe(n)

programme.desc: Conclusion of a two-part storyline. programme.desc: Frank has a rough night thinking about Chris's injuries, Kim and Frank talk about fixing their relationship, and Gavin dumps Lynn very publicly in the Woolpack(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Kathy feels trapped by Chris, Alan wants Amos to be his best man, Jack agrees to get involved with the heritage farm, and Vic sees a looter at the plane crash site(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Denise accuses Don of starting the phone calls again and threatens to go to the police. Alf asks Rita to be his Mayoress, and Emily tells Percy her wedding is off(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Raquel spoils Des on his birthday, Kevin tells Derek that his car is on its last legs, and Jim is amazed when Mike tells him he can borrow the Jag to take Liz to the races(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: Geraldine McEwan makes her last appearance as the spinster sleuth before handing over the knitting needles to Julia McKenzie. Following the death of old acquaintance Mr Rafiel, Miss Marple receives instructions drawn up prior to his demise asking her to embark on a coach tour to investigate an unspecified possible crime. It soon transpires that many of her companions are also travelling at his invitation and they are all in some way connected to a girl who went missing from a nun's sanctuary during World War Two, supposedly to be with her German lover, Rafiel's son Michael. Murder mystery, with a star-studded supporting cast including Richard E Grant, Johnny Briggs, Amanda Burton, Anne Reid, Ronni Ancona, George Cole, Will Mellor and Laura Michelle Kelly(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Conclusion of a two-part storyline. A kidnap case leaves Vernon's plans for Liz and Ben's wedding in chaos, while Helen's dreams of a fresh start for her and Matt look set to be shattered. Jenny is relieved when she finally gets a medical diagnosis explaining her recent symptoms, but the news brings a difficult decision for husband Dennis, who has to choose between his wife and his police career. programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: The doctor becomes convinced something is wrong with him following a conversation with his mother, and Louisa's attempts at getting him to open up about the loss of his father prove futile. Mrs Tishell goes to the surgery for advice regarding a pain in her jaw, only to find herself falling in love with Martin all over again. Elsewhere, Al receives a visit from two military policemen who are trying to track down Mike Pruddy(n)

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: Louisa decides to leave for Spain with baby James and says an awkward goodbye to Martin, but when he finds the scan of her brain, he spots a malformation and realises she could suffer a haemorrhage if she gets on the plane. Will the doctor make it to the airport in time? Meanwhile, Margaret reveals the real reason she has come to Portwenn, and disaster strikes at Bert and Jennifer's engagement party when Caroline receives an electric shock(n)

programme.title: Endeavour
programme.desc: As the sleuth studies for his forthcoming sergeant's exam, he investigates a hit-and-run that claimed the life of an eminent Oxford professor. The victim had been at odds with his peers over the fate of a potentially lucrative parcel of college-owned land, and the case is complicated by the appearance of DI Thursday's old gangland enemy - whom he must face alone while Endeavour makes a trip back to Lincolnshire to visit his ailing father(n)

programme.title: The Protectors
programme.desc: The Contessa and Paul uncover a plot to destroy all members of the organisation but are unable to contact Harry to warn him. Drama, starring Robert Vaughn and Nyree Dawn Porter(n)

programme.title: The Saint
programme.desc: Simon Templar encounters a deadly lady with a gun and an attitude - and soon finds himself caught in the middle of an all-out revolution. Starring Roger Moore(n)

programme.title: Kojak
programme.desc: A woman conspires to have her husband murdered while plotting a diamond heist with her lover. Guest starring Leslie Nielsen and Antonio Fargas (Starsky and Hutch)(n)

programme.title: Quincy ME
programme.desc: Despite all evidence to the contrary, a woman doggedly persists in claiming her murdered son is actually still alive, presenting Quincy with a fresh mystery to solve(n)

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: Arthur unwittingly causes a diplomatic incident by trying to strike a deal with the crew of a visiting Russian ship. Guest starring Rula Lenska, with George Cole and Dennis Waterman(n)

programme.title: The Sweeney
programme.desc: A security guard is murdered during a payroll snatch, and Regan and Carter are called in to support local investigating officers. However, bad blood between the maverick detective inspector and the force's chief superintendent going back several years manifests itself in an old allegation of corruption being rekindled. Richard Griffiths features alongside John Thaw and Dennis Waterman in the final episode of the crime drama(n)

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: Bodie's sanity is questioned when he becomes preoccupied with a gang of Hell's Angels and puts his life in jeopardy by taking part in a motorbike race to the death. Drama, starring Martin Shaw and Lewis Collins(n)

programme.title: The Saint
programme.desc: When a conman and his girlfriend trick a prominent scientist with a tale about hidden treasure, he is taken hostage in Hamburg and sold to the Russians. Templar is 'lured' along similar lines and the confidence trick enables the spy to find the missing man. Roger Moore stars(n)

programme.title: Kojak
programme.desc: A businessman plans to burn down his printing company as an insurance scam. When he kills his partner, he tries to make it look like he died in the fire - but Theo is not fooled(n)

programme.title: Quincy ME
programme.desc: Left stranded by a car accident while enjoying a short break with new girlfriend Barbara, Quincy gets caught up in a race against time to identify a mystery poison before it claims more lives - including that of Sam. Jack Klugman stars(n)

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: Private investigator Caroline Selby seeks Arthur's help in locating eccentric recluse and inventor Albert Goddard, who has come into an inheritance. Vintage comedy drama, starring George Cole and Dennis Waterman(n)

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: An amnesiac woman delivers a garbled message about an attack on a diplomatic meeting planned for the following day. She later regains her memory and tells Bodie and Doyle her employers were abducted by the same gunmen who intend to carry out the action. Gordon Jackson, Martin Shaw and Lewis Collins star(n)

programme.title: The Sweeney
programme.desc: Regan and Carter stake out a dodgy garage, believed to be the base of a criminal gang. John Thaw and Dennis Waterman star in the first episode of the crime-busting drama, with Ian Hendry, Brian Blessed and June Brown(n)

programme.title: The Chase Celebrity Special
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as EastEnders star Charlie Brooks, paralympian Richard Whitehead, comedian Shappi Khorsandi and presenter David Dickinson pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They must work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught(n)

programme.title: Made in Britain
programme.desc: A look at what goes into orange squash and a profile of a family firm making brooms. Plus, an insight into the care that goes into making a Savile Row suit, and how trumpets are handcrafted. Ricky Tomlinson narrates(n)

programme.title: Gordon, Gino and Fred: Road Trip
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay, Gino D'Acampo and Fred Sirieix's European adventure brings them to Fred's cherished homeland of France, where an old friend has asked him to host an oyster festival in Arcachon on the Atlantic coast. As the trio set off across France in search of ingredients, Fred is determined to show his companions the best his country has to offer, with highlights including the famous nudist beaches of the French Riviera - where Gordon attracts unwanted attention - an unruly pony and Fred demonstrating his questionable musical skills(n)

programme.title: Full Metal Jacket (1987)
programme.desc: Raw recruits undergo harsh training under a domineering Marine drill sergeant after being called up for service in the Vietnam War, but his brutal methods lead to the breakdown of one of his cadets. Eventually, some of the fully trained soldiers get their first taste of combat in 1968. Stanley Kubrick's drama, starring Matthew Modine, Adam Baldwin, Vincent D'Onofrio and R Lee Ermey(n)

programme.title: Yorkshire Airport
programme.desc: Cameras focus on life at Leeds Bradford airport during the summer of 2018. It may not be the UK's biggest, but what it lacks in size, it makes up for in ambition. With owners having green-lit a multi-million pound investment, the airport is undergoing a huge makeover. There are also problems for the staff due to an emergency landing after a plane's cockpit warning alarm shows its door is still open. Meanwhile, terminal manager Nathan has to deal with a blocked international arrivals door. Hugh Dennis narrates(n)

programme.title: Yorkshire Airport
programme.desc: The airline auditors pay a surprise visit to Leeds Bradford airport, which leaves baggage boys Jimmy and Michael in fear for their jobs when they manage to lose a piece of luggage. Duty manager Nathan has to deal with a flood in the toilets next to an executive departure lounge, and new recruit Gabby faces a trial by fire at the Departure Gate. She has to tell the members of an irate family that they will not be sitting together on their flight to Palma, and struggles to control the ensuing confrontation(n)

programme.desc: The medical examiner is shot by a mystery gunman while delving into the circumstances of a murder, leaving Sam, Dr Asten and Lt Monaghan to take over the investigation(n)

programme.desc: Bodie's schoolteacher girlfriend seeks CI5's assistance to quash a drug-smuggling ring after learning a 14-year-old pupil is involved having used then hidden a discarded firearm. programme.title: River Monsters
programme.desc: On a remote lake in Malaysia, two men mysteriously vanish - just hours apart. Amid rumours that a giant fish is on the loose, biologist and monster-hunter Jeremy Wade sets out to track down the possible culprit. Could the killer be an interloper from South America, as reports suggest, or is there a home-grown predator that fits the bill? A trail of evidence leads Jeremy into an ancient rainforest, where an old adversary lies in wait. More than just a beast with a gruesome rap sheet, it's also the one river monster that he has thus far failed to catch(n)

programme.title: Assassins (1995)
programme.desc: Hitman Robert Rath is planning to quit his lethal profession, especially when he is stalked by a ruthless younger assassin who has no qualms about killing innocent bystanders. Thriller, starring Sylvester Stallone, Antonio Banderas and Julianne Moore(n)

programme.desc: Hitman Robert Rath is planning to quit his lethal profession, especially when he is stalked by a ruthless younger assassin who has no qualms about killing innocent bystanders. Thriller, starring Sylvester Stallone, Antonio Banderas and Julianne Moore(n)

programme.title: The Saint
programme.desc: Simon Templar encounters a deadly lady with a gun and an attitude - and soon finds himself caught in the middle of an all-out revolution. Starring Roger Moore(n)

programme.desc: A woman conspires to have her husband murdered while plotting a diamond heist with her lover. Guest starring Leslie Nielsen and Antonio Fargas (Starsky and Hutch)(n)

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: Arthur unwittingly causes a diplomatic incident by trying to strike a deal with the crew of a visiting Russian ship. Guest starring Rula Lenska, with George Cole and Dennis Waterman(n)

programme.title: The Sweeney
programme.desc: A security guard is murdered during a payroll snatch, and Regan and Carter are called in to support local investigating officers. However, bad blood between the maverick detective inspector and the force's chief superintendent going back several years manifests itself in an old allegation of corruption being rekindled. Richard Griffiths features alongside John Thaw and Dennis Waterman in the final episode of the crime drama(n)

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: Bodie's sanity is questioned when he becomes preoccupied with a gang of Hell's Angels and puts his life in jeopardy by taking part in a motorbike race to the death. Drama, starring Martin Shaw and Lewis Collins(n)

programme.title: The Saint
programme.desc: When a conman and his girlfriend trick a prominent scientist with a tale about hidden treasure, he is taken hostage in Hamburg and sold to the Russians. Templar is 'lured' along similar lines and the confidence trick enables the spy to find the missing man. Roger Moore stars(n)

programme.title: Kojak
programme.desc: A businessman plans to burn down his printing company as an insurance scam. When he kills his partner, he tries to make it look like he died in the fire - but Theo is not fooled(n)

programme.title: Quincy ME
programme.desc: Left stranded by a car accident while enjoying a short break with new girlfriend Barbara, Quincy gets caught up in a race against time to identify a mystery poison before it claims more lives - including that of Sam. Jack Klugman stars(n)

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: Private investigator Caroline Selby seeks Arthur's help in locating eccentric recluse and inventor Albert Goddard, who has come into an inheritance. Vintage comedy drama, starring George Cole and Dennis Waterman(n)

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: An amnesiac woman delivers a garbled message about an attack on a diplomatic meeting planned for the following day. programme.title: The Sweeney
programme.desc: Regan and Carter stake out a dodgy garage, believed to be the base of a criminal gang. John Thaw and Dennis Waterman star in the first episode of the crime-busting drama, with Ian Hendry, Brian Blessed and June Brown(n)

programme.title: Made in Britain
programme.desc: A look at what goes into orange squash and a profile of a family firm making brooms. Plus, an insight into the care that goes into making a Savile Row suit, and how trumpets are handcrafted. Ricky Tomlinson narrates(n)

programme.title: Gordon, Gino and Fred: Road Trip
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay, Gino D'Acampo and Fred Sirieix's European adventure brings them to Fred's cherished homeland of France, where an old friend has asked him to host an oyster festival in Arcachon on the Atlantic coast. As the trio set off across France in search of ingredients, Fred is determined to show his companions the best his country has to offer, with highlights including the famous nudist beaches of the French Riviera - where Gordon attracts unwanted attention - an unruly pony and Fred demonstrating his questionable musical skills(n)

programme.title: Full Metal Jacket (1987)
programme.desc: Raw recruits undergo harsh training under a domineering Marine drill sergeant after being called up for service in the Vietnam War, but his brutal methods lead to the breakdown of one of his cadets. Eventually, some of the fully trained soldiers get their first taste of combat in 1968. Stanley Kubrick's drama, starring Matthew Modine, Adam Baldwin, Vincent D'Onofrio and R Lee Ermey(n)

programme.title: Yorkshire Airport
programme.desc: Cameras focus on life at Leeds Bradford airport during the summer of 2018. It may not be the UK's biggest, but what it lacks in size, it makes up for in ambition. With owners having green-lit a multi-million pound investment, the airport is undergoing a huge makeover. There are also problems for the staff due to an emergency landing after a plane's cockpit warning alarm shows its door is still open. Meanwhile, terminal manager Nathan has to deal with a blocked international arrivals door. Hugh Dennis narrates(n)

programme.title: Yorkshire Airport
programme.desc: The airline auditors pay a surprise visit to Leeds Bradford airport, which leaves baggage boys Jimmy and Michael in fear for their jobs when they manage to lose a piece of luggage. Duty manager Nathan has to deal with a flood in the toilets next to an executive departure lounge, and new recruit Gabby faces a trial by fire at the Departure Gate. She has to tell the members of an irate family that they will not be sitting together on their flight to Palma, and struggles to control the ensuing confrontation(n)

programme.desc: On a remote lake in Malaysia, two men mysteriously vanish - just hours apart. Could the killer be an interloper from South America, as reports suggest, or is there a home-grown predator that fits the bill? programme.title: Britain's Great Pension Crisis with Michael Buerk
programme.desc: An estimated one in four workers are not saving enough for their retirement, with around 15 million not saving at all. Michael Buerk presents the first of a two-part investigation into Britain's crisis, revealing how many people are facing retirement with no or little money. Across the episodes, one couple have their pension pot opened up to scrutiny as they take on a series of challenges showing how much they can expect when they stop working(n)

programme.title: Critical Surgery: Changing Lives
programme.desc: Every year 20 volunteer medics travel to Ethiopia with the Facing Africa charity, to perform reconstructive facial surgery on patients with little or no health care facilities. The first of two documentaries about their work focuses on 22-year-old Zinash, who has a tumour that has been growing for more than six years. It has now taken over her lower jaw and will kill her if not treated. The Facing Africa medics are her only hope - but this is the first time they have undertaken such a complex operation. Can they save Zinash?(n)

programme.title: Undercover: Nailing the Fraudsters

He also joins a specialist police unit tasked with tackling fraudulent insurance claims as they arrest a woman who claimed to have cancer and faked a miscarriage. Plus, a lead prosecutor shares the evidence from the case of a man who pretended to be in a coma to avoid conviction(n)

programme.desc: Trainer Graeme Hall helps famous faces solve their dogs' behavioural problems, using his experience to find quick but long-lasting solutions. Graeme visits Essex, where actress and presenter Denise Van Outen is being given the runaround by her French bulldog Tilly. They begin with Italian eatery Riobello in Bedford, which is finding it hard to stand out from the town's 40 other Italian concerns. Financial difficulties are driving a wedge between owners Giovani and Amanda, and convincing the couple to cut their enormous menu to the bone to win over the Italian community will be no mean feat(n)

programme.title: Missing or Murdered?
programme.desc: A look at the case of 35-year-old chef Claudia Lawrence, who disappeared in 2009 sparking one of the most high profile missing persons cases of recent years. The programme examines how media coverage of Claudia's private life impacted on the police investigation, and analyses theories of what really happened, including a recent possible sighting of her in Amsterdam and her mother's fears of sex trafficking(n)

programme.title: Criminals: Caught on Camera
programme.desc: Journalist Nick Wallis reveals how police use CCTV in the war against knife crime. In Exeter, a woman comes home to find her boyfriend has been stabbed several times. When he dies on the way to hospital, a murder team is assembled - and the programme reveals how 999 calls, interviews and CCTV footage help solve the horrific case. Meanwhile, in St Helens a camera operator spots an agitated man brandishing a blade and must give a running commentary until armed police arrive(n)

programme.desc: Lachlan Colston, a lieutenant in the Royal Australian Navy, is murdered on US soil, prompting Special Investigator Naomi Parsons to travel to New Orleans from Australia to investigate. Evidence mounts suggesting that Colston's death was a hired hit, but when the team moves in to apprehend the assassin, Parsons' rash actions jeopardise the arrest. Suitably humbled, the Aussie agent agrees to tow the line as Pride and his colleagues learn that Colston may have been killed to derail a nuclear programme to be established on the Cocos Islands. programme.title: NCIS: New Orleans
programme.desc: The Big Easy's annual Red Dress Run is brought to an abrupt halt when participants discover a body on route. Luckily, the NCIS team is competing in the race, and is on hand to investigate. The victim is identified as petty officer Kelsey Weaver, who was last seen running though the city, shouting that she was being pursued by a man with a gun. CCTV footage shows no signs of Weaver's apparent assailant, but when toxicology reports reveal the dead woman had traces of a hallucinogenic drug in her system, Pride and his colleagues are drawn into the battle to stop the spread of this new narcotic substance. Naval crime drama, starring Scott Bakula(n)
