programme.desc: With a nod to the season of goodwill and gifts, the team highlights some of the more innovative products available this Christmas. Georgie Barrat is on cooking duties, aided by a raft of kitchen tech and by Jon Bentley as her sous chef. But will either be of any help with getting Christmas dinner on the table in time? Meanwhile, Ortis Deley and Craig Charles are in charge of putting on a hi-tech party, and have invited round plenty of friends and family - including the UK's oldest identical twins. The gang also acquires the ultimate Christmas tree(n)

programme.title: Funniest Ever Pets at Christmas for Help the Animals
programme.desc: A special episode illustrating members of the animal kingdom enjoying a bit too much Christmas spirit. Full of festive cheer, the edition gathers thoughts and reactions from the nation's favourite animal-loving comedians and celebrities, including Shane Richie, Jo Brand, Susan Calman, Nina Wadia and Michelle Visage. The programme is aired in support of RSPCA, Scottish SPCA, Blue Cross, Donkey Sanctuary and Wildlife Aid Foundation, raising funds for the safety, care and welfare of all the UK's animals, from the tiniest to the most powerful of beasts(n)

programme.title: Cruising with Jane McDonald
programme.desc: The singer enjoys new adventures in the Mediterranean, cruising along the stunning, stylish Riviera. She begins in historic Rome, where she embarks on a vintage car tour of the city. That done, Jane joins her cruise vessel to sail to the tourist hotspots of Florence and Pisa. In Monte Carlo, she samples a hint of the millionaire lifestyle, meeting yacht masters and sneaking into a £8,000-per-night suite in one of Monaco's most exclusive hotels. While in Spain, she tastes tapas from Barcelona's world-famous La Boqueria food market, and then braves the heights of the vertigo-inducing King's Passage(n)

programme.title: Eamonn & Ruth: How the Other Half Lives
programme.desc: Eamonn Holmes and Ruth Langsford discover the most expensive ways in which people can enjoy the season of goodwill, beginning by sampling a £10,000 advent calendar, populated with rare miniature whiskies. Another festive trinket gets a million-pound makeover in the form of the world's first diamond crackers, with each containing a real gem. Eamonn meets the heir to the Cointreau throne to share a classy, festive cocktail, before suiting up to discover what it takes to be a bona fide Santa. As the pair discover, even wrapping paper can go over the top, with sheets smothered in Swarovski crystals costing £20,000 each(n)

programme.title: Fatal Attraction (1987)
programme.desc: A married lawyer has a one-night stand with a business associate while his wife and children are out of town. He hopes to keep his infidelity a secret, but she is determined to continue the relationship, and begins obsessively stalking him and his unsuspecting family. Thriller, starring Michael Douglas, Glenn Close and Anne Archer(n)

programme.title: The Fighting Kentuckian (1949)
programme.desc: French settlers are granted asylum in America in the aftermath of Napoleon's defeat at Waterloo and a Kentucky rifleman finds himself falling for one of the newcomers - a general's daughter. However, he faces a rival for her affections in the form of a ruthless landowner who plans to steal the terrritory her fellow exiles have settled on. George Waggner's Western, starring John Wayne, Vera Hruba Ralston, Philip Dorn and Oliver Hardy(n)

programme.title: Sword of Sherwood Forest (1960)
programme.desc: Legendary outlaw hero Robin Hood rallies his merry men to thwart a dastardly plan by the Sheriff of Nottingham to murder the Archbishop of Canterbury, who has denounced the sheriff's plot to confiscate another nobleman's land. Medieval adventure, starring Richard Greene, Peter Cushing, Niall MacGinnis, Richard Pasco, Sarah Branch and Oliver Reed(n)

programme.title: Last Train from Gun Hill (1959)
programme.desc: A marshal discovers the man responsible for raping and killing his wife is actually the son of an old friend, setting the scene for a showdown. Western, starring Kirk Douglas, Anthony Quinn, Brian Hutton and Carolyn Jones(n)

programme.title: The Return of the Musketeers (1989)
programme.desc: D'Artagnan reunites his former companions to save the queen of France from the scheming Cardinal Mazarin, and embark on a mission to England to prevent the beheading of Charles I. Meanwhile, the daughter of a former vanquished enemy is seeking revenge on the four heroes. Swashbuckling adventure sequel, with Oliver Reed, Michael York, Frank Finlay, Richard Chamberlain, Kim Cattrall and Roy Kinnear, who died during filming(n)

programme.title: The Monuments Men (2014)
programme.desc: Seven experts in fine art are assembled into a military platoon during the Second World War, and given an unusual mission. They are sent into occupied Europe to recover priceless works of art that have fallen into Nazi hands and ensure the masterpieces survive the conflict. Fact-based drama, directed by and starring George Clooney. With Matt Damon, Bill Murray and Cate Blanchett(n)

programme.title: Die Hard 4.0 (2007)
programme.desc: Tough New York cop John McClane is sent to escort a computer hacker to Washington for questioning. During this routine operation he stumbles on a criminal mastermind's plan to launch a technological terrorist attack on the US, and is forced to enlist the hacker's aid in foiling the evil scheme. Action thriller sequel, with Bruce Willis, Timothy Olyphant, Justin Long and Maggie Q(n)

programme.title: Blue Is the Warmest Colour (2013)
programme.desc: A 15-year-old girl falls in love with an older woman and tentatively begins a relationship. They remain together for years to come, as the teenager grows to adulthood and is inspired by her partner to explore her desires and develop her own outlook on the world. Romantic drama, with Lea Seydoux and Adele Exarchopoulos. In French and English(n)

programme.title: The Gunfight at Dodge City (1959)
programme.desc: A gambler becomes the sheriff of a frontier town, planning to use his position to avenge his brother's murderers - but the arrival of a face from the past muddies the water, and leaves him doubting the identity of the killer. Western, starring Joel McCrea, Julie Adams, John McIntire, Nancy Gates and Richard Anderson(n)

programme.title: War of the Wildcats (1943)
programme.desc: An ambitious cowboy engages in a bitter personal feud with a powerful oil baron in a battle for the rights to drill on sacred native American land, and when both men fall for the same woman, the conflict between them gets even uglier. Western adventure, starring John Wayne, Albert Dekker, Martha Scott and Gabby Hayes(n)

programme.title: Arabian Nights (1942)
programme.desc: The story of two half-brothers - one the heroic caliph, the other an evil usurper - as they do battle for the right to rule their kingdom, and win the hand of beautiful dancer Sherazade. Adaptation of the classic adventure tales, starring Jon Hall, Maria Montez, Sabu and Leif Erickson(n)

programme.title: Dakota Incident (1956)
programme.desc: Mismatched travellers, including a bank robber, a politician and a dancing girl, are targeted by Cheyenne braves when their stagecoach is ambushed in the Dakota desert. When the coach is set on fire, they take cover in a gully, and a drawn-out conflict ensues. Western, starring Dale Robertson, Linda Darnell, John Lund and Ward Bond(n)

programme.title: A Beautiful Mind (2001)
programme.desc: Biopic of the brilliant American mathematician John Nash, whose prospects of a promising career with the CIA were confounded by paranoid delusions and schizophrenia as the stress of the job proved too much of a burden. Drama, starring Russell Crowe, Jennifer Connelly, Ed Harris, Judd Hirsch, Paul Bettany and Christopher Plummer(n)

programme.title: The Hitman's Bodyguard (2017)
programme.desc: A disgraced bodyguard is hired to protect a hitman about to testify against the tyrannical president of Belarus at the International Criminal Court in exchange for the release of his wife from prison. Comedy action thriller, starring Ryan Reynolds, Samuel L Jackson, Gary Oldman and Salma Hayek(n)

programme.title: Red (2010)
programme.desc: A retired CIA agent survives an assassination attempt and realises something in his past has made him a target. He sets out to reunite the members of his old hit squad to find out who is trying to kill them and why, but in the process, uncovers dangerous secrets. Action thriller, with Bruce Willis, Morgan Freeman, John Malkovich and Helen Mirren(n)

programme.title: Searching for Ingmar Bergman (2018)

programme.title: The Inn of the Sixth Happiness (1958)

Fact-based drama, starring Ingrid Bergman, with Curt Jurgens, Ronald Squire and Robert Donat(n)

programme.title: David Attenborough's Natural Curiosities
programme.desc: The naturalist continues to explore the secrets behind evolution, this time focusing on the animal kingdom's bizarre hybrids, including the 'pizzly bear', a cross between a polar bear and a grizzly, which is thought to have come into being as a result of global warming. He also covers the killer bee brought into existence due to the transfer of African bees to South America(n)

programme.title: The Life of Mammals
programme.desc: David Attenborough examines the world of rodents, whose constantly growing incisor teeth are used to chisel through any obstacle and range from squirrels and the English harvest mouse to the large South American capybara. He looks at examples of their dental prowess, showing how agoutis in Panama break open rock-hard nutshells and the skills employed by beavers in Wyoming as they fell massive trees to build dams rivalling feats of human engineering(n)

programme.title: Impossible Engineering
programme.desc: The construction of Kansai International Airport upon the largest man-made island in the world, in Osaka Bay, Japan. Pioneering innovators from the past such as Pierre Danel, who developed a system against the pounding sea, and 19th-century mechanic Richard Dudgeon made the project possible. The development of airports including Berlin's Tempelhof Airport and London's Croydon Airport are also explored to reveal how engineers attempted this feat of engineering.(n)

programme.desc: Tony Robinson and the team investigate Gateholm Island, off the coast of Pembrokeshire, where a number of mysterious artefacts were discovered some time ago, suggesting the site was once of religious importance. The only way to get to the island is by rigging a 500-metre zip wire way above the wave-lashed rocks. The archaeologists battle inclement weather to begin a dig on the mainland, where team leader Francis Pryor sees characteristics suggestive of a classic Iron Age fort beginning to emerge(n)

programme.title: Time Team
programme.desc: Tony Robinson and the team descend on Bitterley, Shropshire, where local headmistress Jane Bishop has been leading an exploration of a field, which locals believe used to be the site of houses and streets. The villagers begin to excavate test pits in their own gardens to help the professionals, but it is three days before their answer is revealed(n)

programme.title: War Factories
programme.desc: Henry Ford's plan, with the help of Albert Kahn, to build a giant factory in Michigan, covering 3.5million sq ft and including an aircraft production line half a mile in length(n)

programme.title: World War Weird
programme.desc: How the Japanese air force dropped swarms of fleas on a Chinese city in a bid to infect the population with bubonic plague(n)

programme.title: Impossible Railways
programme.desc: Documentary exploring unusual rail networks from around the world, beginning with a look at trains in cities, including an inverted monorail in Wuppertal, Germany. With contributions from Susana Esteves da Fonseca(n)

programme.title: Time Team
programme.desc: Tony Robinson and the team descend on Bitterley, Shropshire, where local headmistress Jane Bishop has been leading an exploration of a field, which locals believe used to be the site of houses and streets. programme.title: Attenborough's Living Lights
programme.desc: David Attenborough explores the world of bioluminescence, the often spectacular natural light produced by creatures including glow-worms, fireflies and plankton, using specially designed cameras, some fitted to a new generation of deep-sea submersibles and robots to record instances in the very deep ocean. The programme shows a millipede glowing on the forest floor in California, the mesmerising flashing light patterns of the synchronous firefly, the spooky glow of a rare dragon fish and luminous earth worms in France(n)

programme.title: The Life of Mammals
programme.desc: David Attenborough examines the hunting tactics of the world's carnivores. Arctic foxes in the frozen north must collect food during warmer times to survive the winter, while the big cats of the southern hemisphere rely on stealth and surprise to catch each meal. The presenter also reveals how leopards and tigers are solitary hunters, coming together only to mate, whereas lions stay in family groups so they can tackle larger prey and better protect their young(n)

programme.title: Impossible Engineering
programme.desc: Experts analyse the work of engineers including Sir Nigel Gresley, who engineered the world's fastest steam train in 1938 and Eric Laithwaite, whose linear motor made frictionless travel a possibility. These innovators influenced the engineering of the Shanghai Maglev, the fastest passenger train in operation on Earth, which broke numerous engineering boundaries in its construction. Held in place by a series of electromagnets, it levitates on an air gap of 10mm and is able to reach a top speed of 431kms per hour(n)

Student Hannah Charrington, management consultant Veronica Alexander, comedian Frank O'Hare and self-proclaimed 'one-man entertainment system' Bex Carter take turns hosting dinner parties featuring a country house-themed menu, an Italian feast, a TV dinner and an extravagant fancy-dress evening(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: In Cambridge, Tom hosts the first night of a week of dinner parties, and is joined by fresh food fiend Nicole, motor mouth Sam, and retired hairdresser Philip. Each diner shares stories, food and even kick-boxing as they compete for the £1,000 prize money(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: Dick helps Erin and JB fix the rusty hinges on their solarium doors. Tim and Margareeth decide to launch a foodie weekend, and May and Jack prepare the yoga room for guests(n)

programme.title: Undercover Boss USA
programme.desc: Mitchell Modell, CEO of Modell's Sporting Goods, goes incognito among his employees. He poses as a contestant on a fake reality TV show, and tries learn more about the people working for his company, which has been in his family for four generations since its founding by his great-grandfather in 1889(n)

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Chef Gordon Ramsay travels to the El Greco restaurant in Austin, Texas, where the owners have resorted to using a microwave to prepare their 'authentic' Greek dishes. Gordon faces the difficult task of uniting a squabbling family before tackling the bigger problem of a business on the brink of collapse(n)

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Robert grows tired of the lack of privacy at his parents' house and moves out, only to end up living with a couple who are uncannily similar to Marie and Frank(n)

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Jealousy rears its ugly head when his inexperienced colleague Andy achieves Ray's lifelong ambition of having an article published in Sports Illustrated magazine(n)

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: Part one of two. The shrink proffers romantic advice to both Niles and Daphne, hoping that they will admit their mutual affection. However, it remains to be seen whether they will have the nerve to be honest with each other before her impending marriage to Donny(n)

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: Part two of two. As Daphne prepares to tie the knot with Donny, her family arrives for the wedding of the year and Niles struggles with his feelings for her(n)

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Three couples from Berkshire compete to host the best dinner party and walk away with the cash prize of £1,000, with Julian and Lesley, who prepare a meat-heavy menu, Tash and Rob, whose healthy meal has an unwelcome ingredient in the dessert, and Chris and Amanda, who recite their wedding vows during the evening(n)

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Scarlette Douglas helps Pat from Reading, who is searching the south-east coast of Cyprus for a retirement home, with the assistance of daughter Tash(n)

programme.title: Kirstie's Handmade Christmas
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp offers festive tips on how to have a stylish Christmas without breaking the bank(n)

programme.title: The Secret Life of the Zoo
programme.desc: Keepers introduce elderly chameleon Mr Parsons to two new prospective partners, and bongo Malaeka shows little interest in her potential mate, Moti. Meanwhile in the aquarium, George the pinstripe damba is spotted in distress, and keepers notice a lump on his gill which may be affecting his breathing(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau
programme.desc: Following Dick Strawbridge and Angel Adoree as they restore a French chateau that has stood empty for 40 years(n)

programme.title: Caught on Camera
programme.desc: Documentary featuring real-life footage - from the funny to the dramatic - showing what can go wrong both on and off the train tracks. From shocking near-misses and devastating derailments to rush-hour rage and unique on-board entertainment, all captured on cameras and phones and told by the people who witnessed and survived them. Plus, psychologists and industry experts discuss train-related anxieties and situations that passengers could experience in and around the railways(n)

programme.title: What Makes a Murderer
programme.desc: In 1993, Anthony Powell was working as a gang enforcer in south London. One night he was sent to collect a debt that resulted in him shooting dead a man with a sawn-off shotgun. He was convicted of murder and served more than 21 years behind bars. Now, in an episode focusing on the factors behind gang-related crime, Anthony submits himself to biological and psychological tests to determine whether he truly had control over his actions or if a combination of hidden factors made him destined to kill(n)

programme.title: The Family Secret
programme.desc: Documentary about sexual abuse within a family that has been kept a secret for 25 years. The film features access to all members of the family, and documents a meeting between the perpetrator and the victim(n)

programme.title: Our Guy in Japan
programme.desc: Part two of two. Guy visits a slum in Yokohama, where he sneaks into 'love hotels', illegal gambling dens and the yakuza-organised crime gang headquarters. The rest of his offbeat itinerary includes working with the maintenance team on the world's longest, strongest and most expensive suspension bridge, and training under the mystical guidance of one of Japan's last remaining swordsmiths. Guy also makes a special detour to pay his respects at Hiroshima, where he finds the tram that was up and running just three days after a nuclear bomb destroyed the city in August 1945(n)

programme.title: The World's Most Expensive Presents
programme.desc: Cameras follow craftsmen and women producing lavish gifts with price tags to match, including a 24-carat gold-plated bike worth £250,000. There's also a look at the designer who creates the most lavish outfits for dogs - including a £40,000 ball gown(n)

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: The chef again comes to the aid of failing American restaurants, starting in Fishtown, Philadelphia, where he tries to help the three owners of the Hot Potato Cafe, which has not made a profit in two years. Spurred on by a scathing review by an influential critic, he makes radical changes to alter the staff's attitudes and create food that is up to his own exacting standards(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The final visit is to Rivendell Outdoor Club, a clothing-optional resort in Wimborne, Dorset, managed by couple Kerry Murphy and Andy Gautry. On arrival not all the contestants are happy to let it all hang out but a warm welcome from the hosts soon puts them all at ease(n)

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien head to Wokingham in Berkshire to meet professional gardener Mark whose barns are brimful of bikes, bric-a-brac and baggage(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Four amateur cooks from Basildon, Essex, compete to win the £1,000 prize. Advertising sales executive Antonia Price, hairdresser Vicky Hilton, web developer Ryan Brown and career coach and local councillor Andy Baggott take turns hosting dinner parties featuring stripping, nerves and conflict, as well as an Essex-themed evening(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: The first host in Cambridgeshire is science teacher Sam, who is confident his Canadian charm will pave his way to victory, while brand manager Lisa hopes to sway her guests with a night of glamour held at her mansion. Community radio producer Merlin and theatre director Julia are the other contestants in a week of dinner parties featuring uncomfortable Jacuzzi dining, unlikely celebrity lookalikes and unusual games of hide-and-seek(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: Erin and JB launch their chateau business by opening it up for a tea party for the locals, including the mayor. May holds her health and wellbeing retreat using her new yoga studio for the first time(n)

programme.title: Undercover Boss USA
programme.desc: Kat Cole goes undercover among the workforce of her company Cinnabon Incorporated, which serves sweet treats from more than 900 bakeries in 40 countries worldwide. Kat has come a long way after being raised by a single mother who fed her family of three girls on $10 a week. She seeks advice from her old boss, former Hooters CEO Coby Brooks, who also went undercover, in his own organisation. Kat has some lofty goals for Cinnabon to become a worldwide food brand, and wants to make sure that the new products she has put in place are working before she implements her plans(n)

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay heads to Atlanta, Georgia, in an attempt to save a family run smokehouse restaurant plagued by mismanagement and lazy cooking. Michon's owner, Al Wilson, had always hoped he could pass his beloved business on to daughter Natalie, but when he suffered a collapsed lung the handover came about much earlier than expected. Now it's down to Gordon to motivate Natalie and save Michon's from disaster(n)

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Robert and Amy split up and Ray is left to take the blame for making disparaging remarks about marriage. American comedy, starring Ray Romano and Brad Garrett(n)

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Marie discovers she and Frank are not as fit as they thought, so she throws out all their junk food and persuades him to join her on a diet. However, this goes down badly with the rest of the family. Comedy, starring Peter Boyle and Doris Roberts(n)

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: Double bill. Donny issues a lawsuit against Daphne for jilting him at the altar, and Mel plots revenge against Niles after he abandoned his marriage to her. Comedy, starring David Hyde Pierce, Jane Leeves and Saul Rubinek(n)

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Three couples from Reading compete to host the best dinner party. Upstanding couple Liz and David kick off the competition with a menu replicating the first food they ever cooked together. However, prank-loving guests Jo and Steve take centre stage as talk around the table turns a bit 'alternative'. On fashion models Franky and Charli's night, Liz proves to be the wildcard as she reveals how much she's researched their online portfolios. On the final night, Jo cannot stomach what's on her and Steve's own menu. However, it remains to be seen who will walk away with the cash prize of £1,000(n)

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Angie and Neelesh met 30 years ago on the London Underground, and now with retirement on the horizon they think the time is right to buy a holiday home in their favourite Spanish town of Estepona. The couple have their hearts set on a new build property and have asked Laura Hamilton to help find them the perfect one. Laura lines up various options for their maximum budget of £260,000, but will any impress them enough to make an offer?(n)

programme.title: Kirstie's Handmade Christmas
programme.desc: Festive crafters are desperate to win Kirstie's best jumper competition in a show that features tips on how to save and update tired knitwear. Plus, some terrific terrazzo and two seasonal mini-makes that are simple and fun to create(n)

programme.title: Buy It Now for Christmas
programme.desc: Rylan Clark-Neal presents the festive version of the show, in which people with ideas for the perfect Christmas gift pitch their products to an audience of shoppers. They provide focus-group feedback, before representatives from three retailers - Amazon Launchpad, Lakeland and JML - decide whether or not to place an order(n)

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Mark, 32, is brought to resus with a head injury after having an epileptic seizure, and he is sent for an urgent CT scan to assess the damage to his brain. Anastasia, 80, comes to urgent care with a pain in her leg and while she is sent for an X-ray to rule out a blood clot, she shares the story of her arrival to study in England from Nigeria, only for civil war to break out back home. Porter Chris, who's been part of the A&E department for 25 years, shares his insights into the job as he goes about his day(n)

programme.title: Two and a Half Men

programme.title: Nightmare Tenants, Slum Landlords
programme.desc: The buy-to-let documentary series looks back at cases from earlier episodes to find out if matters were resolved or have only got worse. They include a Yorkshire landlord who had to call in eviction specialists to remove tenants from the house he inherited from his grandparents, and a bus driver who managed to win back possession of his home from a nightmare tenant. But just as the eviction was going through, she threatened to call the police(n)

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: Agents Stewart McCracken and Elmore Victor head to Liverpool for what they expect to be a routine repossession, but instead face the most challenging eviction of their careers. Behind the front door, they find a critically ill two-year-old child with major heart problems, who is on constant life support and unable to breathe without a ventilator. Meanwhile, Brian O'Shaughnessy and Del Anglin chase £2,000 worth of debt owed by a business based in Kent, and recover £4,000 from a recycling business in Buckinghamshire. Plus, Stewart and Elmore encounter another family in crisis in Birmingham, where a retired couple who acted as guarantor for their daughter's tenancy are liable to pay over £3,000 after she defaulted(n)

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: Agents Delroy and Kevin face a web of deceit in an Italian delicatessen in south London as they chase a huge debt amounting to £29,000. Meanwhile, Steve and Paul must evict a tenant in north London who has not paid her rent for over 18 months, before heading to Cambridgeshire to make one of their most emotional evictions to date. Plus, Brian and Delroy track down a pensioner in Surrey who owes £15,000 for a business he bought four years ago(n)

programme.title: Neighbours
programme.desc: Ned responds to a threat to his family by returning to the fight club, while Chloe is appalled when Hendrix declares his love for her. Susan is worried by Finn and Elly's closeness(n)

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!

Plus, an acrimonious divorce in Chessington comes to a head when the estranged wife obtains a writ to reclaim the house, and an off-licence owner in Hampshire with unpaid rent of more than £5,000 battles to keep his business open just as everything shuts down for the holidays(n)

programme.desc: McCoy discovers two suicide victims were participating in clinical trials of an experimental antidepressant, prompting him to charge the drug company's CEO with second-degree murder. programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit

programme.title: Fit to Rule: How Royal Illness Changed History
programme.desc: Lucy Worsley investigates the medical histories and fraught family dynamics of the monarchy in the 19th and 20th centuries, looking at how the death of Princess Charlotte led to a frantic race to produce a legitimate heir. programme.title: Malta Story (1953)
programme.desc: The island of Malta comes under siege during the Second World War, as both Allied and Axis forces see it as vital to their supply lines. A British reconnaissance pilot arrives on the island, and plays a crucial role in defending it from the advancing enemy forces. Drama, starring Alec Guinness, Anthony Steel, Jack Hawkins, Muriel Pavlow and Flora Robson(n)

programme.title: I'm a Stranger (1952)
programme.desc: An Englishman comes to India to claim his inheritance, only to find his grandfather's will has gone missing. To discover what has become of it and acquire his rightful fortune, he needs the help of a detective, a window cleaner - and movie star Greta Gynt. Comedy, starring Patric Doonan and James Hayter, with Gynt playing herself(n)

programme.title: Love Birds
programme.desc: Short documentary about one of London's last working class pigeon racing communities(n)

programme.title: Walks Around Britain
programme.desc: Andrew White walks along part of an old railway line on the Isle of Man, and travels between the Sun and Pluto pubs in North Yorkshire(n)

programme.desc: A property expert offers advice to British householders aiming to buy a second home abroad(n)

programme.title: Property Ladder

programme.title: Trust Me, I'm a Doctor
programme.desc: Chris van Tulleken aims to sort fact from fiction as he investigates whether garlic, watermelon and beetroot really deserve the reputation they have gained as 'superfoods'. Michael Mosley also visits leading nutrition experts to find out whether sugar is really as toxic as tobacco, as had been suggested by recent studies. Plus, Dr Saleyha Ahsan explodes some of the myths surrounding the best way to treat burns(n)

programme.title: Make Bradford British
programme.desc: The sociological experiment concludes with the participants being paired up to learn about each other's backgrounds. A black man shows a retired policeman the estate where he once suffered a racially motivated attack, and a landlady invites a Muslim woman to work in a pub where the locals challenge her over her traditional attire. A welder comes away with a changed view of Islam after visiting one of the city's 80 mosques, while a self-confessed feminist invites taxi driver Mohammed to a classic British dinner party and questions him about his cultural views on women(n)

programme.title: Bronco Bullfrog (1970)
programme.desc: A teenage couple craving freedom from their disapproving families fall in with a young criminal fresh out of borstal, who wants their help with a robbery. Drama, starring Del Walker and Anne Gooding(n)

programme.title: Away (2016)
programme.desc: In Blackpool, an unlikely friendship blossoms between a lonely, suicidal widower and a young woman trying to escape an abusive ex-boyfriend. Drama, starring Timothy Spall, Juno Temple and Matt Ryan(n)

They work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught(n)
